props.title: Workflow Engine Features
props.description: Workflow Engine gives you every building block needed to design, version, and execute workflows of any complexity — visually, programmatically, or via REST.

props.subtitle: Benefits
props.title: Why choose Workflow Engine
props.description: With Workflow Engine, it's never been easier to add a fully operational workflow automation solution into your existing .NET / .NET Core system. Our ready-made engine and comprehensive documentation let you get down to development faster, and hit the market sooner.

features.title: Customizable
features.text: Vendor-provided source code — customize and make changes without any assistance.

features.title: Professional customer support
features.text: Over the past year we have implemented 95% of customers' requests for new functionality.

features.title: Requires no special training
features.text: Any developer can write a Proof of Concept. Visual Designer can be embedded in a webpage — making it easy to use for non-technical users.

features.title: Perpetual license
features.text: Pay just once and use the current version forever. One-year updates subscription included.

props.subtitle: Designer
props.title: HTML5 Visual Designer
props.description: We realize that not everyone is a developer. That's why Workflow Engine comes with a built-in HTML5 visual designer that lets you create interactive workflows of any complexity — no need to draft them programmatically. Design, model, edit, and visualize your custom workflows right in the browser.

features.title: Drag & Drop
features.text: Drag & drop your workflow activities to the desired location on the canvas without losing connections, or add new transitions between activities.

features.title: Fully adapted for business uses
features.text: Workflow Designer is a simple and user-friendly tool that allows any user to change routes.

features.title: Full customization
features.text: You can easily change any element of the Designer — for example, the form controls.

props.subtitle: Core components
props.title: The building blocks of every workflow
props.description: Workflow Engine offers its users all the components necessary to implement workflows of any complexity; and helps them to maintain consistent business processes. Workflow Engine is designed to improve organizational efficiency and productivity, allowing companies to concentrate on job performance rather than workflow management.

features.title: Activities
features.text: Define statuses and the set of methods executed when a process enters that state — the atomic steps of every workflow.

features.title: Transitions
features.text: Connect activities with rules that move the process forward — triggered by commands, timers, or conditional branches.

features.title: Actions
features.text: Execute custom code during an activity — call APIs, update data, send notifications, or any other side effect the process requires.

features.title: Actors
features.text: Assign users, groups, or systems to roles in the process. Route tasks to the right participants and enforce responsibility at every step.

features.title: Commands
features.text: Trigger specific transitions from the UI or API. Commands expose the actions a user or system can take on a process in its current state.

features.title: Timers
features.text: Schedule transitions by interval or absolute date/time. Drive SLAs, escalations, reminders, and any time-bound behavior declaratively.

testimonials.text: Workflow Engine provides a flexible and extensible architecture, a powerful workflow engine and an intuitive visual workflow designer that enables us to rapidly modify our application to meet the needs of our clients.
testimonials.name: Mark West
testimonials.title: CEO

props.subtitle: Version control
props.title: Track changes. Roll back when needed.
props.description: Workflow Engine comes with built-in version control that helps you keep track of any changes introduced to your workflows and revert them when needed. Whenever you update a workflow, the engine versions your changes, letting you choose whether you want existing processes to finish running the old version or to force them to run the new one.

image.alt: Version control scheme — how updated workflows propagate to running processes

props.subtitle: Parallel workflows
props.title: Run branches in parallel — then merge.
props.description: Workflow Engine lets you split a process into multiple branches that execute concurrently. Each branch runs its own activities, and when all of them complete, the workflow merges back into a single flow and continues. Model complex business logic without sacrificing clarity or performance.

image.alt: Parallel workflow scheme — branches executing concurrently and merging on completion

props.subtitle: Workflow essentials
props.title: Everything a process needs at runtime
props.description: Versioning, concurrency, scheduling, and interop — built-in, not bolted on.

features.title: Version Control
features.text: Every change to a workflow is versioned. Decide whether existing processes finish on the old scheme or migrate to the new one — rollback is always available.

features.title: Parallel Workflows
features.text: Split a workflow into branches that execute concurrently. When all branches complete, they merge back and the process continues.

features.title: Timers
features.text: Attach custom timers to transitions — intervals in ms / seconds / minutes / hours / days, or specific date/time triggers. UTC by default, custom formats supported.

features.title: XML Import / Export
features.text: Workflows are stored as versioned XML definitions. Export for backup and sharing; import to reuse schemes across projects and environments.

features.title: Localization Support
features.text: Localize states, command names, and input parameters into any language, right from the Designer toolbar. No code required.

features.title: BPMN 2.0 Support
features.text: Model your processes in any BPMN 2.0 tool and execute them inside Workflow Engine — letting analysts and developers work in the notation they already know.

props.title: Runs on your .NET stack
props.description: Workflow Engine comes in a .NET build, enabling you to run it in any environment that supports these technologies and integrate it into your .NET solutions. Plus, the engine is supplemented with a set of providers that allow you to connect it to a DBMS of your choice.

props.subtitle: Platform & integration
props.title: Runs where your stack already is
props.description: Deploy into any .NET environment, integrate via component or REST, and plug in the security layer you already use.

features.title: Compatibility
features.text: Runs on .NET / .NET Core — any environment that supports the runtime. Ships with providers for Microsoft SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle, MySQL, MongoDB, Azure SQL, and Redis.

features.title: Integration Options
features.text: Embed as a .NET component, or run Workflow Server and call via REST API. The Designer can be embedded natively or via iframe when styles need isolation.

features.title: Your Own Security Layer
features.text: Workflow Engine ships with no embedded security — by design. Plug in whatever auth and access control model your application already uses, without fighting hardcoded rules.

props.subtitle: Integration options
props.title: Two ways to integrate
props.description: Pick the path that fits your application — embed as a .NET component, or run Workflow Server and talk to it over HTTP.

features.title: Server-side integration
features.text: We have prepared detailed instructions on how to integrate Workflow Engine into your .NET or .NET Core project. If there is no possibility to integrate Workflow Engine into your project, just launch Workflow Server and call methods via HTTP API.

features.title: Designer integration
features.text: Use our step-by-step instructions to integrate Workflow Designer into your application. If you are having trouble with styles, use integration via IFrame. If you are using Angular, check one of the following examples: Angular Test, Angular Boilerplate.

props.title: Want a deeper look?
props.description: Talk to our team about fit, licensing, and integration for your project.
